scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope incident handling stayed tied to ticket, tenant, logs, owner action, validation, monitoring, status communication, and completion evidence.
Incomplete tenant scope, environment, client approval, logs, access owner, and retrieval hooks correctly forced an awaiting-evidence exception state.
Legal, editorial, privacy, contractual, and final client signoff requests were refused and routed to adjacent owners.
Webhook, content queue, and observability conflicts produced rework and escalation actions with retest evidence requirements.
Completion without test, rollback, approval, validation, implementation, and retention evidence was refused.
Confidentiality and client-delivery conflict was blocked until approved recipient, channel, scope, data-use authority, and clearance evidence existed.
Identity-provider dependency failure produced a cross-team incident summary with impact, blocked operations, evidence, and owner routing.
Webhook schema and connector change-readiness was held because testing, rollback, client-impact, change-window, and owner approval evidence were missing.
Tenant ID, workspace, entitlement, API, webhook, batch run, feature flag, SLO, status page, and communication terms were normalized into lane-specific evidence and next action.
Production code, MFA bypass, finance, account, and contractual decisions were rejected or routed to adjacent specialists.
Screenshot-only evidence with missing tenant facts, platform policy, runbook, logs, and source context produced low-confidence escalation instead of unsupported diagnosis.
Conflicting tenant runbook, vendor guidance, contract terms, and client instruction were preserved and escalated without confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold for platform operations execution, exception handling, completion evidence, client workflow, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
